productive_behavior: The prespecified plan passed independent planning audit and deterministic plan lint without repair.
Independent Python and R executions recovered the exact planted mean difference, Welch statistic, confidence interval, and Hedges g with clean stderr.
The task-specific reconciliation recorded both all_pass and reconciliation_passed as true, bound each language artifact by SHA-256, and kept every numerical delta below 1e-6.
Reader-facing table precision was repaired while the full-precision Python and R JSON artifacts remained immutable.

After two pathological portrait renders, later repairs produced compact figures with distinct group centers, visible quantitative effect ticks, a visible null, and correct estimate and interval geometry.
release_blockers: The first figure was 2593x4593 with a 65.4% internal blank band because an effect annotation was placed at an arbitrary data-space y coordinate outside the tickless effect panel; Matplotlib emitted 'Tight layout not applied', but the deployed warning matcher recognized only a different warning phrase.
A later 2833x8472 replacement had a 78.9% internal blank band despite clean stderr, proving that plotting warnings alone cannot guard canvas occupancy.
Two report revisions falsely said the confidence interval crossed zero even though the authoritative interval was [4.071144, 5.928856].
Gemma did not identify either extreme blank-canvas defect or the false interval description; it twice returned a nominal visual pass missing the required annotation-data clearance token.

A compact, manually acceptable repair was needlessly rejected after OCR hallucinated the low-confidence fragment 'ole' from colored points and a black error bar and treated it as annotation/data overlap.

The final repair wrote a four-column CSV header but five fields in multiple inferential rows. Deterministic validation correctly rejected the nonrectangular table after the fourth and final repair, leaving the run at requires_human_decision without accepted display registration or independent review.

The evaluator initially failed the exact Python/R and effect-size checks because it accepted hedges_correction_J only under effect_size, not the deployed primary.hedges_correction_J path. The immutable artifacts both match the exact reference after this schema correction, improving the score from 12/18 to 14/18 without changing the four genuine terminal failures.
implemented_repairs: Deterministic figure validation now treats tight-layout and constrained-layout failure variants as blocking, and generation guidance keeps annotations in axes-fraction coordinates or bounded point offsets.
A bounded raster-geometry check rejects an extreme internal blank band separating visible content regions even when the plotting engine emits no warning.
Figure captions and alt text are checked against authoritative machine-readable confidence-interval bounds for false zero/null-crossing claims.
Annotation/data overlap blocking now requires high-confidence OCR, preventing the observed 73%-confidence point-cluster hallucination while preserving ambiguous cases for Gemma review.
CSV/TSV files under output/tables are strictly parsed during post-execution inspection; nonempty headers and rectangular rows are required before an artifact can become successful evidence.
The known-effect evaluator accepts the deployed primary.hedges_correction_J schema and retains exact reference-value checks.
disposition: Rejected at requires_human_decision. The statistical computation and reconciliation were exact, but display generation repeatedly produced pathological layouts, the report misstated interval/null geometry, a low-confidence OCR false positive consumed a repair, and the final reader table was malformed. Each observed defect is now represented by deterministic regression coverage or stricter generation/output validation.
